qa_pairs.question: What is the purpose of the CCDB portal?
qa_pairs.answer: The CCDB portal is where users must register to access resources hosted across Canada by the Alliance and its regional partners, known as the Alliance Federation. It also serves as a gateway to account and allocation information for Advanced Computing Platforms managed through the Digital Research Alliance of Canada (the Alliance).

qa_pairs.question: Who makes up the Alliance Federation?
qa_pairs.answer: The Alliance Federation is collectively formed by the Alliance and its regional partners, who host resources across Canada.

qa_pairs.question: What type of information does CCDB provide access to?
qa_pairs.answer: CCDB provides access to account and allocation information for Advanced Computing Platforms managed through the Digital Research Alliance of Canada.

qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible to register for a CCDB account?
qa_pairs.answer: Any faculty member at a CFI-eligible Canadian university is eligible to register. Researchers from government, non-profits, and industry may also be eligible to use Federation resources, and inquiries for these individuals should be directed to accounts@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What can a registered faculty member do regarding other researchers?
qa_pairs.answer: Once registered, a faculty member can sponsor students, research staff, and collaborators working on their research projects.

qa_pairs.question: Who should government, non-profit, or industry researchers contact about using Federation resources?
qa_pairs.answer: These researchers should direct their inquiries to accounts@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: When do these specific registration instructions apply?
qa_pairs.answer: These instructions only apply if you are registering with the Alliance for the first time.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I already have a CCDB account, even if it is deactivated?
qa_pairs.answer: If you already have a CCDB account, even if it is deactivated, you do not need to apply for a new account again. You should read the Account Renewals FAQ instead.

qa_pairs.question: How many classifications of user roles are there in the CCDB?
qa_pairs.answer: The CCDB has 12 classifications of user roles.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find descriptions and characteristics of CCDB user roles?
qa_pairs.answer: You can visit the User Roles page for descriptions and key characteristics of each available role.

qa_pairs.question: Who must register with the CCDB first, Principal Investigators or Group Members?
qa_pairs.answer: Principal Investigators (PIs) must register with the CCDB first.

qa_pairs.question: How long does the approval process for a Principal Investigator's CCDB registration take?
qa_pairs.answer: The approval process for a Principal Investigator's registration can take up to 2 business days.

qa_pairs.question: What is a CCRI?
qa_pairs.answer: A CCRI is a Role Identifier that is included in the confirmation email sent after a CCDB registration has been approved.

qa_pairs.question: When can Group Members register for a CCDB account?
qa_pairs.answer: Once a PI has their CCRI, all of their Group Members can register in the CCDB.

qa_pairs.question: What is the main difference in the registration process for Group Members compared to PIs?
qa_pairs.answer: Group Members must indicate their sponsor's CCRI and do not need to provide information about the research area, unlike PIs. All Group Members must also be approved by a sponsor.

qa_pairs.question: Who approves Group Members' registration applications?
qa_pairs.answer: All Group Members must be approved by a sponsor.

qa_pairs.question: How do sponsors confirm Group Member applications?
qa_pairs.answer: Sponsors will receive an email requiring them to confirm each Group Member once the Group Member has completed the first step of the registration process. New sponsored role requests pending for approval will also show on the CCDB home page of the PI.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if I have questions about getting a CCDB account?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have any questions about getting an account, you should email accounts@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What is the first step to creating a CCDB account?
qa_pairs.answer: The first step is to go to the login page.

qa_pairs.question: Which link do I click to start the registration process on the login page?
qa_pairs.answer: You need to click on the 'Register' link, which is located underneath the Login and Password fields, next to the 'Forgot Password' link.

qa_pairs.question: What agreements must I read and accept before beginning the CCDB registration process?
qa_pairs.answer: You must read and accept the 'Policies, Standards, Terms, and Procedures', 'Consent to receive optional news and communications from the Digital Research Alliance of Canada', and 'Optional transfer of file ownership on account deactivation'. 'Consent to Access User Data' is optional.

qa_pairs.question: Is consenting to 'Access User Data' mandatory for CCDB registration?
qa_pairs.answer: No, accepting the Consent to Access User Data is optional.

qa_pairs.question: What might happen if I do not consent to 'Access User Data'?
qa_pairs.answer: Not consenting to this agreement may limit the Alliance's ability to provide the support required in certain cases.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I read the policies mentioned during registration?
qa_pairs.answer: You can read the policies by visiting the [https://alliancecan.ca/en/policies page].

qa_pairs.question: What type of email address should I use when completing the CCDB registration form?
qa_pairs.answer: You should register with your institutional email address.

qa_pairs.question: What happens after I click 'Submit application' during account creation?
qa_pairs.answer: After clicking 'Submit application', you will receive an email.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I do not receive the confirmation email after submitting my application?
qa_pairs.answer: If you do not receive the confirmation email, you should contact accounts@tech.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What is the next step after receiving the confirmation email for CCDB registration?
qa_pairs.answer: Once you have received your confirmation email, you need to click on the confirmation link indicated in the email. If the link does not work, copy and paste the confirmation link into your browser.

qa_pairs.question: How is a PI's account request processed after they click the confirmation link?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are the PI, an administrator will process your account request, checking your submitted information to ensure you are faculty at your specified institution. Your account request will be processed within 2 business days.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if I am a Group Member after clicking the confirmation link?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are a Group Member, an email will be sent to your Sponsor asking them to confirm your application.

qa_pairs.question: What should I remember to do once my CCDB account request has been approved?
qa_pairs.answer: Once your request has been approved, you will receive a notification email and should note your CCRI.

qa_pairs.question: What does a PI need to do with their CCRI once it's approved?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are a PI, you will need to provide your CCRI to all Group Members that you will be sponsoring.

qa_pairs.question: How can I retrieve my CCRI if I forget it?
qa_pairs.answer: If you forget your CCRI, you can log in to the system and retrieve it.

qa_pairs.question: As a PI, after registration, what should I do regarding Group Members?
qa_pairs.answer: As a PI, all of the Group Members you want to sponsor should register for CCDB accounts, and you will need to confirm every individual who registers with CCDB using your CCRI.

qa_pairs.question: Can Group Members log in to the CCDB immediately after completing the first step of registration?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Group Members will only be able to log in to the CCDB once the Sponsor has approved their application request.

qa_pairs.question: How does a Sponsor confirm a Group Member's application after the Group Member has completed Step 1?
qa_pairs.answer: The Sponsor will receive an email with a confirmation link. New application requests will also appear on the CCDB home page with the same link. Clicking either link will direct the Sponsor to a confirmation page.

qa_pairs.question: What information should a Sponsor verify when approving a Group Member's application?
qa_pairs.answer: The Sponsor should check the accuracy of the information provided by their Group Member, ensuring it is correct and that the specified email is the Group Member’s institutional email.

qa_pairs.question: What happens after a Sponsor clicks the 'Confirm this role' button?
qa_pairs.answer: After clicking 'Confirm this role', both the Sponsor and the Group Member will receive a confirmation email, and a confirmation screen will be displayed to the Sponsor.
